item.title: Bitcoin Fortress вАУ BF220 - Top Bitcoin News - 6/14/26
item.description:
Date: June 14, 2026
Block Height: 953666
Moscow Time: 1564
Fees: 1 / 1 /1

¬†

In this episode:

In Episode 220 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we examine the most important Bitcoin developments from June 7вАУ14, 2026. We discuss the return of ETF inflows, the growing dominance of BlackRock and Fidelity in the Bitcoin ETF market, StrategyвАЩs latest Bitcoin purchase and the evolving treasury-company landscape, mining profitability and the recent difficulty adjustment, stablecoin regulation under the GENIUS Act, the ongoing CLARITY Act debate, and the broader implications of AI, surveillance, and digital freedom. We also explore the major themes emerging from the Bitcoin podcast universe, including inflation, private-company Bitcoin treasuries, operational security, and post-quantum Bitcoin research. Finally, we feature A Bitcoin Way of Life вАУ Week 4, reflect on Bitcoin as a discipline rather than merely an investment, and close with a reminder that while the fiat world grows increasingly complex, Bitcoin continues to do exactly what it was designed to doвАФproduce blocks and provide a foundation for financial sovereignty.

¬†

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast вАУ The Praxeology of Privacy: Freedom Tech vs Mass Surveillance | Max Hillebrand
item.description:
Privacy isnвАЩt about having something to hide. ItвАЩs about preserving the freedom to act, communicate, and coordinate without coercion.

In this episode of THE Bitcoin Podcast, Walker sits down with privacy advocate, cypherpunk, and Freedom Tech builder Max Hillebrand to discuss his new book, The Praxeology of Privacy.

Max explains how Austrian economics and cypherpunk philosophy intersect, why privacy is a natural consequence of self-ownership and property rights, and how surveillance distorts economic calculation in much the same way that money printing distorts markets.

The conversation explores:

  • Why privacy is fundamentally tied to freedom
  • The economic consequences of surveillance
  • CBDCs and the future of financial control
  • Why corporations and governments increasingly operate as surveillance partners
  • The role of Bitcoin in creating a parallel economy
  • Tor, VPNs, encryption, and network-level privacy
  • NostrвАЩs growing ecosystem and why developer adoption matters more than user counts
  • Operational security (OpSec) in both the digital and physical world
  • SignalвАЩs limitations and the future of decentralized private communications
  • White Noise, Marmot, and the next generation of unstoppable encrypted messaging
  • How Freedom Tech can make coercion more expensive and liberty more accessible

Max also shares his vision for a future where money, communication, identity, and publishing are all decentralized, permissionless, and resistant to censorship.

Whether youвАЩre new to privacy or already deep down the cypherpunk rabbit hole, this conversation provides a powerful framework for understanding why privacy mattersвАФand what practical steps you can take to reclaim it.

FOLLOW MAX:

NOSTR: https://primal.net/maxhillebrand

Try White Noise Chat: https://www.whitenoise.chat/

https://towardsliberty.com/

GET HIS BOOK:

The Praxeology of Privacy on Amazon: https://tinyurl.com/2xn9bs8y

Read on Nostr: https://tinyurl.com/4b42kb7d

PARTNERS & DISCOUNTS:

  • LEDN: Bitcoin-backed lending. Go to ledn.io/walker and unlock liquidity WITHOUT selling your bitcoin.
  • BLOCKSTREAM JADE: BLOCKSTREAM JADE HARDWARE WALLET: Head to https://store.blockstream.com/ and use coupon code WALKER for 10% off!

item.title: Bitcoin Magazine Podcast вАУ Calle on Bitchat: Messaging When The Internet is Shut Down
item.description:
What happens to communication when the internet gets shut off? Frank Corva sits down with Calle вАФ creator of Cashu and a contributor to BitChat вАФ to unpack two of the most exciting open-source projects in Bitcoin. They dig into how Cashu ecash matured from experimental tech into a real economy, and why BitChat's Bluetooth mesh network took off in Jamaica, Iran, and Nepal. Calle also explains why ecash may become the native money of AI agents.



Grab your copy THE 2036 ISSUEрЯСЗhttps://store.bitcoinmagazine.com/pages/the-2036-issue

рЯФґ Frank Corva - Independent Content ProducerрЯФґ Creator of Cashu and contributor to BitChat



Chapters:


00:56 вАФ The state of Cashu ecash and building the foundation

02:07 вАФ Who's actually adopting ecash and why

03:26 вАФ Ecash as "pocket money" for AI agents

04:50 вАФ BitChat goes global: Jamaica, Iran, Nepal

05:35 вАФ Why "build it and they will come" isn't a law

07:45 вАФ Staying connected when the internet is shut down

09:00 вАФ How far BitChat's mesh network can really reach

11:32 вАФ Nostr, geohash chats, and going online

12:46 вАФ What's next: open-source AI agents



#Bitcoin #Cashu #BitChat #Ecash #Calle #MITBitcoinExpo #BitcoinPrivacy #MeshNetworking #Nostr #OpenSource #Bluetooth #AIagents #BitcoinMagazine #Satoshis #Privacy #DecentralizedTech #FreeSpeech #LightningNetwork #Meshtastic #BitcoinNews #SelfSovereign #BitcoinLayer2 #ZeusWallet #OfflineMessaging


DISCLAIMER: The views and opinions expressed in this show are those of the participants and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of BTC Inc., Bitcoin Magazine, or any affiliated entities. This content is provided for informational and educational purposes only and should not be construed as investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice. Nothing contained in this show constitutes a solicitation, recommendation, endorsement, or offer to buy or sell any securities or financial instruments. Viewers should consult their own advisors before making financial or business decisions.

item.title: The Bitcoin Treasury Podcast вАУ The Hidden Cost of a Large Bitcoin Buy
item.description:
The most common mistake in a first Bitcoin allocation is not the price paid. It is assuming the execution will take care of itself.

Teams make the buy decision, line up the approval, and then route a seven-figure order through the same retail venue they would use to buy a few hundred dollars of Bitcoin. The order fills, but not at the price they saw. The gap between the quoted price and what they actually paid can be significant, and on a large order it is real money.

Buying Bitcoin is operationally straightforward. Buying it well, at size, is not.

Michael Geraci, derivatives trader at Secure Digital Markets, covers the exchange, execution, and OTC relationships institutional Bitcoin holders need to understand before the first dollar moves. Will Reeves, CEO of Fold, joins with the operatorвАЩs view from running a public company that holds Bitcoin on its own balance sheet.

вАЬAt a million dollars and up is really where the OTC markets offer depth and liquidity.вАЭ¬† вАФ Michael Geraci, Secure Digital Markets

What this session covers

When a retail exchange stops being enough

  • ¬† ¬† Past a certain size, execution cost becomes real. Slippage becomes material around $250K in notional and is unambiguous past $1M, where retail order books cannot absorb the trade cleanly.
  • ¬† ¬† A $1M Bitcoin buy typically runs 5 to 60 bps in execution cost. On a single trade that looks small; across a recurring accumulation program it is the difference that decides your cost basis.
  • ¬† ¬† OTC desks exist to solve this, offering real depth, bilateral pricing, and a counterparty accountable on the other end of the phone.

How a large order actually gets worked

  • ¬† ¬† Size is worked, not clicked. A meaningful order is sliced across venues and over time rather than dropped into a single book that it would move against itself.
  • ¬† ¬† Execution is benchmarked. Working an order against TWAP or VWAP lets it track the market instead of running ahead of it, which is the difference between a fill a treasurer can defend to a board and one they cannot.
  • ¬† ¬† Discretion matters. Bilateral pricing keeps a large order off the public tape, so the trade does not signal its own size to the rest of the market.

The operational groundwork that makes execution clean

  • ¬† ¬† Tier-1 banking relationships matter. Fiat needs to move cleanly, and wires to crypto venues are routinely flagged or blocked at banks that are not set up for them. Established banking rails remove that friction.
  • ¬† ¬† Work with a firm that picks up the phone. Setting up to trade at size involves real onboarding, banking, and compliance questions, and the answers are specific to each entity. A client-centric desk walks you through them with a named person on the other end of the line, rather than leaving you to a support inbox or a contact form.
  • ¬† ¬† Settlement speed is an edge. Pre-funded trades that settle T+0 to T+1 reduce the window in which anything can go wrong, rather than leaving funds in transit for days.
  • ¬† ¬† Flexibility is the point of a desk. Bespoke, bilateral structuring gives a treasury options a public order book cannot, from how an order is filled to how a position is later hedged or financed.

Get these pieces right and the first allocation stops being a leap of faith. The price you pay is defensible, the execution is clean, and you have a counterparty who answers when you call. From there, the same desk can help the position do more than sit, structuring yield, downside protection, and financing against Bitcoin you already hold, which is exactly the role a desk like SDM is built to play.

About Secure Digital Markets

SDM is a crypto-native institutional brokerage offering spot execution, derivatives, and structured lending. It specializes in bilateral transactions that give treasuries the depth, flexibility, and privacy a retail exchange cannot match, while clients retain custody of their own assets. You can learn more at SDM.co.

]]>

What this session covers

When a retail exchange stops being enough

  • ¬† ¬† Past a certain size, execution cost becomes real. Slippage becomes material around $250K in notional and is unambiguous past $1M, where retail order books cannot absorb the trade cleanly.
  • ¬† ¬† A $1M Bitcoin buy typically runs 5 to 60 bps in execution cost. On a single trade that looks small; across a recurring accumulation program it is the difference that decides your cost basis.
  • ¬† ¬† OTC desks exist to solve this, offering real depth, bilateral pricing, and a counterparty accountable on the other end of the phone.

How a large order actually gets worked

  • ¬† ¬† Size is worked, not clicked. A meaningful order is sliced across venues and over time rather than dropped into a single book that it would move against itself.
  • ¬† ¬† Execution is benchmarked. Working an order against TWAP or VWAP lets it track the market instead of running ahead of it, which is the difference between a fill a treasurer can defend to a board and one they cannot.
  • ¬† ¬† Discretion matters. Bilateral pricing keeps a large order off the public tape, so the trade does not signal its own size to the rest of the market.

The operational groundwork that makes execution clean

  • ¬† ¬† Tier-1 banking relationships matter. Fiat needs to move cleanly, and wires to crypto venues are routinely flagged or blocked at banks that are not set up for them. Established banking rails remove that friction.
  • ¬† ¬† Work with a firm that picks up the phone. Setting up to trade at size involves real onboarding, banking, and compliance questions, and the answers are specific to each entity. A client-centric desk walks you through them with a named person on the other end of the line, rather than leaving you to a support inbox or a contact form.
  • ¬† ¬† Settlement speed is an edge. Pre-funded trades that settle T+0 to T+1 reduce the window in which anything can go wrong, rather than leaving funds in transit for days.
  • ¬† ¬† Flexibility is the point of a desk. Bespoke, bilateral structuring gives a treasury options a public order book cannot, from how an order is filled to how a position is later hedged or financed.

Get these pieces right and the first allocation stops being a leap of faith. The price you pay is defensible, the execution is clean, and you have a counterparty who answers when you call. From there, the same desk can help the position do more than sit, structuring yield, downside protection, and financing against Bitcoin you already hold, which is exactly the role a desk like SDM is built to play.

About Secure Digital Markets

SDM is a crypto-native institutional brokerage offering spot execution, derivatives, and structured lending. It specializes in bilateral transactions that give treasuries the depth, flexibility, and privacy a retail exchange cannot match, while clients retain custody of their own assets. You can learn more at SDM.co.

]]>

item.title: bitcoin ist - Wissen statt FOMO вАУ 18) ... Deflation f√Љr alle
item.description:
Warum schlagen √Цkonomen und Zentralbanken sofort Alarm, wenn sie das Wort вАЮDeflationвАЬ h√ґren? Sie warnen vor Wirtschaftskrisen, hoher Arbeitslosigkeit und dem Zusammenbruch der M√§rkte. Und in unserem aktuellen Finanzsystem haben sie damit sogar recht вАУ allerdings tun sie damit dem Begriff der Deflation unrecht.

In dieser Folge von вАЮbitcoin ist...вАЬ schauen wir uns den Begriff der Deflation genauer an. Denn es gibt zwei Arten von Deflation: Eine, die ein krankes System kollabieren l√§sst, und eine, die der absolute Normalzustand der menschlichen Zivilisation sein sollte.

  • Der "Fehler" im System: Warum unser schuldenbasiertes Geldsystem Angst vor sinkenden Preisen hat und warum Kredite niemals вАЮteurerвАЬ werden d√Љrfen.

  • Die gute Deflation: Wie der technologische Fortschritt (vom Rad bis zum Internet) unser Leben nat√Љrlicherweise immer effizienter und g√Љnstiger machen m√Љsste.

  • Das 2%-Mantra: Warum Zentralbanken die Geldmenge k√Љnstlich ausweiten, um den Produktivit√§tsgewinn deiner Lebenszeit unbemerkt abzusch√ґpfen.

  • Bitcoin als L√ґsung: Wie eine absolut begrenzte Geldmenge von 21 Millionen Einheiten daf√Љr sorgt, dass der menschliche Fortschritt endlich wieder direkt in deinem Geldbeutel landet.

вАЮDie Zentralbanken bek√§mpfen die schlechte Deflation, indem sie permanent Geld drucken. Doch damit zerst√ґren sie gleichzeitig die gute Deflation вАУ sie fressen den technologischen Fortschritt einfach auf und verw√§ssern unsere Kaufkraft schleichend.вАЬ

Alles von "bitcoin ist..." Hier geht es zu allen meinen Kan√§len: https://linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Einsteiger-Spezial: m√ґchtest du wissen, WIE man bitcoin sicher kauft und verwahrt? kostenlose und sichere Kaufanleitung direkt in dein Postfach

Hinterlasse mir gerne eine 5-Sterne-Bewertung oder einen Kommentar. Danke, Dein Christian.


]]>

item.description:

https://www.thebitcoinway.com/guides/panama?utmsource=tbw&utmmedium=fountain&utmcampaign=planb&utmcontent=banter

вАФ------------------------

War, inflation, and growing government surveillance have made a вАЬPlan BвАЭ residency more essential than ever. In this special edition of Bitcoin Banter, Michael and Tony sit down with entrepreneur Adam Hudson to count down the top 5 reasons you should get a second residency, citizenship, or passport abroad. This episode breaks down how to build real freedom and financial sovereignty.

Drawing on AdamвАЩs own journey from Australia to Panama, the conversation covers offshore residency, second passports, territorial tax systems, self-custody Bitcoin, privacy and cybersecurity, and how everyday people (not just the rich) can create an exit plan in an increasingly restrictive world. Whether youвАЩre an Australian, a Bitcoiner, or anyone seeking more freedom and optionality, this is your roadmap to getting started.

item.title: State of Bitcoin with Brandon Keys вАУ The European Bitcoin Ban Hiding In Plain Sight with Matyas Kuchar - State of Bitcoin Ep. 238
item.description:
Until last year, more than 1,000 restaurants in Czech Republic accepted Bitcoin. Then it all stopped.

The MiCA regulation didn't ban Bitcoin in Europe. It did something far more effective вАФ it made the rails impossible to operate. And as the Czech merchant economy quietly disappeared, two bigger forces started moving in the background: the petrodollar dying, and CBDCs coming online in Europe THIS YEAR.

In this episode, I sit down with Matyas Kuchar вАФ co-founder of BTC Prague, the largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe, and one of the most thoughtful voices on European Bitcoin policy вАФ to break down why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe, why the European surveillance state is spreading faster than Americans realize, and why he believes Bitcoin must win because the alternative is just so much worse.

You'll learn:

вЖТ How MiCA quietly killed 1,000+ Bitcoin-accepting restaurants in Czech Republic вАФ and why it wasn't even a direct ban

вЖТ Why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe (Trezor, Slush Pool, BIP-39, General Bytes вАФ all started in Prague)

вЖТ Why the petrodollar is dying вАФ and what the loss of US hegemony means for global decentralization

вЖТ Why Europeans use Bitcoin as money while Americans treat it as digital gold вАФ and which approach is right

вЖТ The European CBDC threat: pilots are launching THIS YEAR вАФ and why most Americans haven't heard about it

вЖТ The CBDC mechanism in Matyas's words: "You can analyze, you can control, you can surveil, you can send from a helicopter"

вЖТ Why even an anarcho-capitalist now meets with politicians вАФ and what changed his mind

вЖТ Why Bitcoin is no longer just savings вАФ it's the network of people building the future you actually want to live in

рЯЪ® JOIN ME AT BTC PRAGUE 2026

The largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe вАФ June 11-13. Use code GREENCANDLE for discounted tickets:

https://btcprague.com/

рЯОѓ PROTECT YOUR BITCOIN WITH SALT LENDING

Borrow against your Bitcoin without selling a single sat. Green Candle viewers get an exclusive offer:

https://saltlending.com/green-candle

рЯОѓ PLAN YOUR WEALTH WITH STRONG WEALTH

Build a Bitcoin-aligned wealth strategy вАФ estate planning, tax optimization, portfolio construction:

https://strongwealth.net/

рЯОѓ MINE BITCOIN WITH SIMPLE MINING

First-year repairs free for Green Candle listeners:

https://simplemining.io/green-candle

рЯУК REFERENCED IN THIS EPISODE

вАҐ MiCA regulation (EU 2023/1114) вАФ Markets in Crypto-Assets framework

вАҐ Czech Republic Bitcoin tax exemption (3-year hold rule)

вАҐ Slush Pool (founded 2010 in Prague, first ever Bitcoin mining pool)

вАҐ BIP-39 seed phrase standard (developed in Prague)

вАҐ Trezor hardware wallet (first ever, founded in Prague)

вАҐ EU CBDC pilot programs (launching 2026)

вАҐ BTC Prague 2026 conference вАФ June 11-13

Bitcoin #BTCPrague #Matyas #MiCA #BitcoinEurope #CBDC #BitcoinPolicy #EuropeanBitcoin #BitcoinConference #SoundMoney #Petrodollar #BitcoinAdoption #CzechRepublic #FinancialFreedom #BitcoinNews

рЯФФ SUBSCRIBE for weekly Bitcoin, macro, and personal finance content.

вЪ†пЄП Disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research.

item.title: BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast вАУ Bitcoin und Deutschland: Wo stehen wir und wo kann die Reise hingehen? #6 mit Larry
item.description:
Herzlich willkommen zum BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast(https://www.bitbucks.de)!

Heute haben wir einen besonderen Gast: Larry, bekannt als @B1tcoin3r auf X. Larry ist ein erfahrener Bitcoin-Experte, Vortragsredner und Workshop-Host. Dank seines weitreichenden Netzwerks in f√Љhrenden Positionen bringt er tiefgehende Einblicke in die aktuelle Bitcoin-Situation in Deutschland mit. Gemeinsam sprechen wir √Љber die deutsche Bitcoin-Politik, die Auswirkungen der neuen Regierung und die m√ґgliche Zukunft von Bitcoin in Deutschland.

Viel Spa√Я beim Zuh√ґren!

Zudem reden wir auch noch √Љber die Themen:

- Die aktuelle Bitcoin Lage in Deutschland (Adaption, Beliebtheit)

- L√ґsungen f√Љr eine Bitcoin Adaption

- Einfluss der Politik auf Bitcoin

- Ob Bitcoin die Politik √Љberhaupt braucht- Bitcoin und Unternehmen bzw. Start-Ups

- Wie viele Jahre es dauern wird, bis Deutschland Bitcoin als offizielles Zahlungsmittel einf√Љhrt

- Bitcoin St√§dte Spiel: Errate die f√Љnf beliebtesten Bitcoin St√§dte weltweit

- Wie Bitcoin Larrys Leben ver√§ndert hat

- Glaskugel: Bitcoin Preis am Ende des Jahres 2025Podcast Folge auf Spotify:

---------------------------------------------------------------------

https://www.bitbucks.de/Apple Download: https://apps.apple.com/de/app/bitcoin-wallet-bitbucks/id1453167599

https://www.instagram.com/bit_bucks/

https://www.tiktok.com/@bitbucks

https://x.com/bit_bucksPodcast auf Spotify:

https://open.spotify.com/show/0bhDawE23ld6lPw7KnqUH5?si=5182b2525e7144db

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Vernetz dich gerne mit den Rednern :)

LinkedIn Konsti Schwemlein: https://www.linkedin.com/in/konstantin-schwemlein/

--------------------------------------------------------------

TimeStamps:

00:00 Intro

01:15 Wie bist du zu Bitcoin gekommen?

03:20 Wie steht es um Bitcoin in Deutschland?

07:45 Thema Bitcoin: Bev√ґlkerung vs. Unternehmen/Politik

12:46 Was sind deine Verbesserungsvorschl√§ge?

item.title: Bitcoin Audible вАУ The TRUTH about Iran's "Bitcoin" seizure
item.description:
I break down the TreasuryвАЩs claim that it seized $1B of IranвАЩs "crypto" and explain why this was actually Tether, not Bitcoin вАУ and why that distinction matters.

I digs into how stablecoins, ETFs, and exchanges recreate the same centralized, confiscable banking system, contrasted with true Bitcoin self-custody.

When you consider Iran, Russia, and Afghanistan as case studies, it is clear that every other reserve asset becomes politicized. The writing is on the wall for centralised finance, and the world will be forced onto Bitcoin because it is the only neutral money that simply keeps working.

item.title: M√Љnzcrime - True Crime Bitcoin Podcast вАУ Unter Geldw√§scheverdacht Episode 6 (mit Plep4Fun)
item.description:
Konto gesperrt. Komplett. Kein √Ьberweisen, kein Abheben, kein gar nichts. Und das ohne Vorwarnung, ohne Erkl√§rung, einfach so. 


Genau das hat Pleb4Fun im Januar 2025 erwischt. Von einer Sekunde auf die andere war er raus aus dem System вАУ w√§hrend Rechnungen weiterhin kamen und das Leben sich nicht um sein Bankproblem scherte. 
Was dann folgte, war kein schneller Anruf mit happy ending. Es war ein b√Љrokratischer Alptraum, der eine viel unbequemere Frage aufwirft: Wie leicht ger√§t man heutzutage eigentlich ins Visier der Beh√ґrden, obwohl man absolut nichts falsch gemacht hat? 


Chefermittler Mavi hat sich mit Pleb4Fun zusammengesetzt und die ganze Geschichte auseinandergenommen вАУ akribisch, ehrlich und ohne Sch√ґnf√§rberei. Raus gekommen ist dabei eine Episode, die unter die Haut geht. Nicht weil sie rei√Яerisch ist, sondern weil es Realit√§t ist.


Geldw√§scheverdacht. Ein Begriff, den die meisten Menschen mit Kriminellen verbinden. Nicht mit jemandem, der einfach seinen Alltag lebt. 
Das hier ist keine abstrakte Diskussion √Љber Finanzgesetze. Das ist die Geschichte eines Menschen, der pl√ґtzlich beweisen musste, dass er kein Verbrecher ist вАУ und das Protokoll dazu liegt jetzt auf dem Tisch. 

Links zur Folge:
Pleb4Fun Dokumentation auf X

Ticketverkauf Event Brivat (Bitcoin Privacy Event)

Der Fall Samourai

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews вАУ Syed, Midnight President вАФ $200M Spent, Zero VCs, and Why Anonymity Isn't Privacy
item.description: <![CDATA[Fahmi Syed runs the Midnight Foundation, custodian of a fourth-generation privacy-first blockchain backed by Charles Hoskinson. Roughly $200 million spent. Zero VC funding. One of the biggest airdrops in crypto history.Jamie Redman sits down with Fahmi at Consensus 2026 for the full breakdown: programmable privacy, selective disclosure, the dual-token Night/Dust model, why Zcash and Monero keep getting delisted, why agentic AI desperately needs a proof layer, and what "Web 2.5" actually means.We cover:- The bank-account-number anecdote that defines rational privacy- Why transparent blockchains can't carry institutions across the bridge- Midnight's private-permissions model between Monero and JPMorgan- The dual-token Night/Dust design and predictable corporate costs- One of crypto's biggest airdrops вАФ no VCs, no insider allocations- March 2026 mainnet and the 100+ builders in pre-prod- The 2008 TradFi trading floor that shaped his self-custody viewsFilmed at Consensus 2026.Host: Jamie Redman]]>

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin вАУ Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 13: Die stille Steuer вАУ Warum der Staat Inflation nicht bek√§mpfen will
item.description:
Deine Eltern haben dir gesagt: Leg Geld zur√Љck. Sparen lohnt sich.

Guter Rat вАУ damals. Aber heute ist etwas kaputt.

In Folge 12 haben wir gezeigt, warum dein Kontostand stabil aussehen kann und du trotzdem √§rmer wirst. Heute gehen wir einen Schritt weiter und stellen die unbequeme Frage dahinter: Warum wird Inflation nicht einfach abgestellt?

Die Antwort: Weil das System genau so gebaut ist.

DAS LERNST DU IN DIESER FOLGE

вАУ Warum Staaten gigantische Schulden durch Inflation heimlich entwerten вАУ Was finanzielle Repression bedeutet вАУ und warum das kein Verschw√ґrungsmythos ist вАУ Wer wirklich die Rechnung zahlt: die stille Steuer auf deine Ersparnisse вАУ Warum das 2%-Ziel der EZB keine zuf√§llige Zahl ist вАУ Was Bitcoin als mengenm√§√Яig begrenztes System damit zu tun hat

WEITERF√ЬHRENDE EMPFEHLUNGEN

Der Bitcoin-Standard вАУ Saifedean Ammous Schulden: Die ersten 5000 Jahre вАУ David Graeber Reinhart & Sbrancia: The Liquidation of Government Debt (Studie, englisch)

VOR-ORT-KURS вАУ 30. MAI 2026

Bitcoin f√Љr Einsteiger вАУ digitales Geld f√Љr analog Geborene. Kein Trading. Kein Hype. Einfach ein ruhiger, verst√§ndlicher Einstieg. https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin

Hinweis: In dieser Folge wird neben meiner Stimme eine KI-generierte Expertenstimme f√Љr technische Erkl√§rpassagen eingesetzt. Alle Inhalte werden von mir recherchiert und gepr√Љft.

вЪ†пЄП Keine Finanzberatung. Ausschlie√Яlich Bildung und pers√ґnliche Perspektive.

]]>

Sie diskutieren die Bitcoin-Adoption, die Adopting Bitcoin-Konferenz, die Sicherheitslage, die allt√§gliche Nutzung von Bitcoin und die politische Situation unter Pr√§sident Bukele.

item.title: Epicenter - Learn about Crypto, Blockchain, Ethereum, Bitcoin and Distributed Technologies вАУ Why Only 3 Builders Control All of Ethereum
item.description:
In this episode, host Friederike Ernst is joined by Kubi Mensah, CEO and co-founder of Gattaca, the company behind Titan Builder. Kubi sheds light on the highly competitive and often opaque world of Ethereum block building, explaining how Gattaca evolved from a centralized exchange proprietary trading firm to one of the three dominant builders responsible for constructing the vast majority of Ethereum blocks today .
¬†



¬†
They dissect the true "journey of a transaction," revealing why over half of all Ethereum transaction value bypasses the public mempool in favor of private order flow auctions and MEV-protection services . Kubi explains the intricate mechanics of top-of-block bidding by high-frequency DeFi arbitrageurs, the necessity of extreme latency optimization, and the "flywheel effect" that makes block building a natural oligopoly . Finally, the discussion turns to the future of the Ethereum roadmap, unpacking how upcoming upgrades like ePBS (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and FOCIL (Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists) aim to permanently alter the power dynamics between block builders, validators, and originators .
¬†
¬†
Topics


  • 04:15 Transitioning from TradFi Trading to Ethereum Block Building
  • 09:30 Redefining the Builder: Relays and Order Flow Auctions
  • 15:00 Unpacking the Mempool: Public vs. Private Transactions
  • 21:45 The Anatomy of a Block & "Top-of-Block" Arbitrage
  • 27:10 How Builders Pay Proposers to Win Auctions
  • 35:20 The Oligopoly: Why Only 3 Builders Dominate Ethereum
  • 42:15 Trust in the Dark Forest: Handling Searcher Bundles
  • 49:00 The Convergence of Builders and Solvers
  • 55:30 The Impact of ePBS, FOCIL, and Pre-confirmations




Sponsors:



Lido V3 introduces stVaults: a modular staking infrastructure that lets builders and institutions deploy custom staking vaults, while staying anchored to stETH as a shared liquidity layer.Get started building with Lido V3 today: https://lido.fi/stvaults?mtm_campaign=epicenterBlock Space Forum: https://blockspace.forum/

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update вАУ Bitcoin, Oil, Saylor and SpaceX: The Weekly Market Shock
item.description: <![CDATA[This week on The Weekly, David Sencil and Graham Stone break down a major set of stories moving Bitcoin and crypto markets.They discuss the reported Iran-US peace deal, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, falling oil futures, and BitcoinвАЩs move above $66K. They also unpack Michael SaylorвАЩs controversial 32 BTC sale, his BTC Prague comments, MetaplanetвАЩs move into Bitcoin yield products, Bitcoin miners pivoting toward AI, AnthropicвАЩs Fable export-control controversy, and the SpaceX IPOвАЩs impact on crypto-based pre-IPO price discovery.A fast-moving episode covering macro, Bitcoin, AI, miners, IPOs, and the next stage of crypto market structure.]]>

They also unpack Michael SaylorвАЩs controversial 32 BTC sale, his BTC Prague comments, MetaplanetвАЩs move into Bitcoin yield products, Bitcoin miners pivoting toward AI, AnthropicвАЩs Fable export-control controversy, and the SpaceX IPOвАЩs impact on crypto-based pre-IPO price discovery.A fast-moving episode covering macro, Bitcoin, AI, miners, IPOs, and the next stage of crypto market structure.]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Rails | Isabel Foxen Duke вАУ BIP 110 & The Future of Soft Forks | SUPER TESTNET
item.description:
One of Bitcoin's most prolific experimental developers, Super Testnet is known for building software at the frontier of Bitcoin research вАФ including notable contributions to BitVM, Drivechains, Lightning, and numerous prototypes that have pushed the boundaries of Bitcoin's design space.

More recently, he's become a prominent participant in discussions surrounding BIP 110, a proposed soft fork intended to restrict transaction patterns associated with Ordinal inscriptions.

While Super broadly agrees with the proposal's underlying goals, he argues its current restrictions are overly broad and could impact "legitimate" monetary uses of Bitcoin.

These concerns led him to share a User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal last week, creating a rare case study in how Bitcoin's consensus rules evolve in practice.

In more detail, Super Testnet & I discuss:

  • BIP 110, and why developers disagree over transaction filtering
  • Soft fork activation through a historical lens
  • Miner-Activated and User-Activated Soft Forks + the hybrid strategy proposed by BIP 110 advocates
  • Super's User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal and its game theoretical implications for BIP 110 activation
  • Novel signaling mechanisms for BIP 110 + their implications for the future of hashrate markets

This episode of Bitcoin Rails is brought to you by my sponsors:

LayerTwo Labs вАФ developing research, software, and technologies for scaling Bitcoin via the integration of Drivechains (BIP 300/301)

Hashi on Sui Network вАФ a primitive for executing Bitcoin Defi transactions, without having to trust a federated bridge or other centralized entity

BitBox вАФ an open-source Bitcoin-only hardware wallet, with smooth UX and no compromises on security. Check out Bitbox [dot] swiss and use code BITCOINRAILS to get a discount

]]>

Im Gespr√§ch mit Relai-CEO Julian Liniger erz√§hlt Leon von seiner Motivation, ein Bitcoin-Buch zu schreiben, weshalb ihn Bitcoin nach √Љber 10 Jahren immer noch so motiviert, wie er die Lage rund um Bitcoin auf der Bilanz sieht, und wohin Bitcoin in den kommenden Jahren noch gehen wird.

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin вАУ To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>

item.title: BTC-ECHO Recap | Top-Stories zu Bitcoin & Kryptow√§hrungen вАУ Wir sind umgezogen!
Krypto Corner вАУ Bitcoin, ETF und NFT: Sicher investieren - Krypto verstehen und sicher starten вАУ Episode #2: Not your keys, not your coins вАУ Wie du nicht alles verlierst
item.description:
Die Berliner WG hat verstanden, was Bitcoin ist. Doch nun steht die wichtigste H√Љrde f√Љr jeden Krypto-Einsteiger an: Wo liegt eigentlich mein Geld, wenn es keine Bank mehr gibt?

In dieser Episode sprechen wir √Љber das Thema Nummer eins, bei dem Anf√§nger die gr√ґ√Яten (und teuersten) Fehler machen: Sicherheit. Max erkl√§rt am K√Љchentisch, warum wir bei Kryptow√§hrungen unsere eigene Bank sind und welche immense Verantwortung das mit sich bringt.

In dieser Folge erf√§hrst du:¬†вАҐ Warum eine вАЮWalletвАЬ eigentlich gar keine Coins enth√§lt, sondern nur Schl√Љssel. вАҐ Den Unterschied zwischen dem Public Key (deiner Kontonummer) und dem Private Key (deinem Tresorschl√Љssel). вАҐ Was eine Seed-Phrase ist und warum diese 12 oder 24 W√ґrter √Љber alles entscheiden. вАҐ Die wichtigste, goldene Regel der Krypto-Welt, gegen die du niemals versto√Яen darfst.

Diese Episode basiert auf Kapitel 2 des Einsteiger-Romans вАЮKrypto verstehen вАУ sicher starten. Band 1вАЬ von Sascha Swen Heiter. Verantwortung steht √Љber Spekulation. Bleib sicher und investiere mit K√ґpfchen!

рЯУЪ¬†Das Buch zur Show:¬†Wenn du das gesamte Wissen sofort anwenden m√ґchtest вАУ von der Einrichtung deiner Hardware Wallets √Љber die Nutzung von Burner Wallets bis hin zu wichtigen rechtlichen und steuerlichen Themen in der DACH-Region вАУ hol dir das komplette Buch! рЯСЙ¬†https://amzn.eu/d/06DuLLUw

рЯОІ¬†Krypto Corner Podcast:¬†H√ґrst du diese Folge gerade auf YouTube? Du findest den¬†Krypto Corner¬†Podcast auch auf Spotify, Apple Podcasts und √Љberall sonst, f√Љr regelm√§√Яige Deep-Dives und Sicherheits-Updates f√Љr unterwegs!

рЯСЗ¬†Unterst√Љtze den Kanal:¬†Wenn dir diese Folge geholfen hat, lass ein Abo da, gib dem Video einen Daumen nach oben und aktiviere die Glocke. Fragen oder Anmerkungen zum zweiten Kapitel? Ab damit in die Kommentare!

вЪЦпЄП¬†Rechtliche Hinweise / Haftungsausschluss:¬†Die in diesem Podcast geteilten Inhalte dienen ausschlie√Яlich der allgemeinen Information sowie der Unterhaltung und stellen¬†keine Anlage-, Rechts- oder Steuerberatung¬†dar. Investitionen in Kryptow√§hrungen sind hochriskant und k√ґnnen zu einem Totalverlust des eingesetzten Kapitals f√Љhren. Jeder H√ґrer und Zuschauer handelt absolut eigenverantwortlich. Besonders bei steuerlichen Themen ist zu beachten, dass die steuerliche Behandlung von Kryptow√§hrungen sehr komplex ist. Bitte konsultiere f√Љr die aktuell geltende Rechtslage in der DACH-Region zwingend einen qualifizierten Steuerberater. Eine Haftung f√Љr Sch√§den, die aus der Nutzung der hier bereitgestellten Informationen entstehen, wird ausdr√Љcklich ausgeschlossen.


#Podcast #KryptoCorner #NotYourKeysNotYourCoins #Krypto #KryptoVerstehen #Bitcoin #KryptoAnf√§nger #Finanzwissen #Blockchain #HardwareWallet #PrivateKeys #Kapitel2

]]>
